concerns.text: The plan cites 'Lamblador/IRSpectra' (test split) as the source for IR spectra to align with QM9. However, the QM9 dataset contains ~130k molecules, while the 'test' split of a typical HuggingFace dataset is often small (<1k). If the IR-Spectra dataset lacks the full molecular coverage of QM9, the inner join on InChIKey will result in a drastically reduced sample size (potentially <1% of QM9), making the planned 130k-scale training infeasible. The plan must verify that the cited IR-Spectra source contains the full population of QM9 molecules or a sufficiently large subset to support the statistical power required for the research question.

The plan assumes a direct 1:1 alignment via InChIKey between QM9 and the cited IR-Spectra dataset. QM9 properties are computed at the B3LYP/6-31G(2df,p) level. If the cited IR-Spectra dataset was generated using a different DFT functional or basis set, the spectral features will not correspond to the target properties (mu, alpha, gap) in the QM9 dataset. This creates a 'wrong dataset' scenario where the predictor (spectrum) and outcome (property) belong to different physical simulations, invalidating the training signal. The plan must confirm the DFT methodology of the IR-Spectra source matches QM9 or explicitly account for the domain shift.
